name.en: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

applicable_situations: Discrimination against a person with disability in employment, education, or public services
Denial of reasonable accommodation in workplace, school, or transport
Refusal to provide accessible infrastructure (ramps, signage, accessible documents) by a public establishment
Violation of reservations in education or government employment for persons with benchmark disability
forum: State Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities; District Court for civil matters; Special Court for offences
limitation_period_note: No fixed limitation for filing a complaint with the State Commissioner. Civil claims follow the Limitation Act (typically 3 years). Criminal complaints under the Act should be filed promptly.
procedural_steps: File written complaint with the State Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities
Commissioner has powers of a civil court: summons, evidence, recommendations
Recommendations to be acted upon by the public authority within prescribed time
For aggrieved party: civil suit in District Court for damages or specific performance
Criminal offences (denial of benefits, fraudulent claim of disability): police complaint and prosecution in Special Court
required_documents: Disability certificate from competent medical authority
Written complaint with details of discrimination or denial
Documentary evidence (denial letter, photographs of inaccessible premises, witness statements)
